title: Paraboea leporina
taxonomy: Paraboea leporina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the genus Paraboea, which consists of several species of tropical plants. Paraboea leporina is native to Southeast Asia and is often found in shaded, humid environments. This plant is a type of herbaceous perennial, with characteristics typical of the Gesneriaceae family. Further classification of Paraboea leporina may include its order and family, which are likely to be Lamiales or a more specific subclass within the Gesneriaceae family.

intro: Paraboea leporina, also known as the "Rabbit Ear Plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Paraboea leporina include iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Paraboea leporina is often used to make infusions, teas, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
